creator: freebsd@beardz.net
text:
On 02/11/2011 14:51, cs@FreeBSD.org wrote:
> Synopsis: [MAINTAINER] irc/weechat-devel: update to 20111023 (0.3.7-dev)
>
> State-Changed-From-To: open->feedback
> State-Changed-By: cs
> State-Changed-When: Wed Nov 2 14:50:57 UTC 2011
> State-Changed-Why:
> There is an option called DEBUG which is not further used in the Makefile. What is it for?
>
> http://www.freebsd.org/cgi/query-pr.cgi?pr=161966

As explained in the previous update for this port ( ports/161520 ) :

[quote]
The port uses cmake and WITH_DEBUG is handled in bsd.cmake.mk.

Please see
http://www.freebsd.org/cgi/getmsg.cgi?fetch=28231+0+/usr/local/www/db/text/2011/cvs-ports/20110417.cvs-ports
[/quote]

Someone needs to fix portlint wrt cmake, so that updaters don't get 
asked this same question every single time we submit an update PR for 
cmake based ports that utilise DEBUG.

Also, please refrain from removing bug-followup@ from the recipient list 
when responding to a PR, thanks.

Regards,

Jase Thew.
